response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented senior developer with genuine strengths in testing, mentoring, and low-latency systems work in DLang, but he clearly misses the core minimum qualifications for this role. Professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments against a hard requirement of 8 years, and C++ does not appear in his skills list. His large-scale distributed-systems exposure (about two years at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) falls short of the 5-year infrastructure requirement, and he has no formal technical-leadership role leading cloud or DevOps engineers, making him a poor fit for a C++-centric cloud compute platform tech lead position.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second)
Strong testing and mentoring track record: authored test frameworks, ran workshops, courses, and coding dojos
Broad full-stack, DevOps, and Kubernetes experience plus long-running open-source platform ownership as a founder
concerns: Roughly 7 months of professional C++ work versus the required 8 years; C++ absent from his stated skills
About 2 years of large-scale distributed-systems experience against the 5-year infrastructure requirement
No formal technical-leadership role managing priorities and mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers across teams
